text:
1
CONNECTING
THE WORLD
FOR THE PEOPLE
 
AGENDA
JANUARY 29 TO
FEBRUARY 2, 2024

2
 Participants Arrival in Panama City.
 Check-in at the Marriott Panama Hotel.
12:00 p.m. a 5:00 p.m.- Open Registration at the Marriott Panama Hotel (Amador Room).
Optional: Book business meetings.
 Participants Arrival in Panama City.
8:00 a.m. – 4:00 p.m. - Open Registration at the Marriott Panama Hotel (Amador Room).
 Lunch on your own.
1:30 p.m. - Meet at the Hotel Lobby for Optional tour to Panama City’s Casco Viejo.
2:00 p.m. - Optional: Welcome Tour Through Panama City’s Casco Viejo.
Experience the historical, charming and vibrant district of Casco Viejo, which is a UNESCO Cultural World 
Heritage Site, and explore the main tourist spots that are stimulating economic growth and attracting 
tourism. Additionally, participants have the option to explore the Mola Museum, which is a tribute to the 
artistry of the Kuna (Guna) indigenous community. Free tours can be arranged directly with the museum 
at the entrance.
Note: Previous registration for this activity is required through the following link by Wednesday, January 24th, 2024.
6:00 p.m. - Optional: Return to the Hotel Marriott Panama Hotel. 
Participants may wish to stay longer and return to the Hotel on their own.
Panama is Open for Partnerships 
Panama is Open for Partnerships 
SATURDAY, JANUARY 27 
SUNDAY, JANUARY 28
JANUARY 29 TO
FEBRUARY 2, 2024

3
Explore Partnerships with Panama’s Logistics Sector
MONDAY, JANUARY 29
 6:00 a.m. - Breakfast at the 
 Marriott Panama Hotel
 7:15 a.m. - Meet at the Hotel Lobby
 7:30 a.m. - Transfer to the 
 Panama Canal
 8:00 a.m. - Visit the Miraflores Locks of 
the Panama Canal, One of Panama City’s 
most impressive marvels of engineering 
See how ships transit through the locks of the canal, learn about 
the operation and movement of this marvel of engineering. A 3D 
movie will be projected to explain the history and operation of the 
Panama Canal. 
 9:10 a.m. - Transfer to the
 International Maritime University of 
Panama
 9:40 a.m. - Explore Collaboration with 
the International Maritime University of 
Panama (UMIP)
• 9:50 a.m. Welcome from Vice Minister Otero.
• 9:55 a.m. Welcome remarks from Víctor Luna Barahona
Rector of UMIP.
• Transport and Logistics Panel
Ilya Espino de Marotta, Deputy Administrator of the Panama 
Canal. 
Carlos Urriola, President, SSA Terminal.
Raffoul Arab, General Manager of Tocumen International Airport.
Tayra Ivonne Barsallo Z, General Director of the National 
Authority for Customs.
Moderator: Rafael Cigarruista, Merchant Marine General Director - 
Panama Maritime Administration.
• 10:45 a.m. - ACE Panama Group Photo. 
 
Let us capture a memorable moment under the Bridge of the 
Americas. The start of hundreds of opportunities of cooperation 
for the benefit of our countries, regions, and communities.
 11:00 a.m. - Transfer to the 
 UMIP Simulators 
 11:15 a.m. - Explore the Simulators
Submerge yourself in the machine simulator, nautical chart 
simulator, liquid cargo simulator and navigation simulator to 
experience first hand how the UMIP cadets are trained. 
 12:10 p.m. - Transfer to the 
 Marriott Panama Hotel 
 12:30 p.m. - Ice Breaker Activity 
 (Albrook Convention Center Room)
 1:15 p.m. - Working Lunch at the Marriott 
Panama Hotel (Albrook Convention Center Room)
 
Delegation will have time for a change of attire (formal business attire for 
the evening reception). 
 2:10 p.m. - Meet at the Hotel Lobby
 2:20 p.m. - Transfer to 
 Panama Digital Gateway
 2:35 p.m. - Connect with the Panama Digital 
Gateway, the first “Green Data Center” to 
position Panama as a Digital Hub in the 
Americas 
Take the opportunity to learn and find new partners working in this neutral 
carrier hub of the latest generation with top technologies and infrastructure. 
It was built following the strictest anti-seismic criteria and designed to 
guarantee the highest standards of reliability and performance.
 3:45 p.m. - Transfer to the Bolivar Palace
• 4:00 p.m. Arrival at Centenario Park, walk to the Bolivar Palace. 
(approximately 200 meters to 650 feet).
• 4:30 p.m. Group Photo in Plaza los Libertadores. 
 
 5:00 p.m. - Welcome to Panama 
“A Hub of Hubs” - Official Welcome Reception 
at the Bolivar Palace 
Official remarks by the Government of Panama, the Organization of 
American States and the Government of the United States.
Janaina Tewaney Mencomo, Minister of Foreign Affairs, Panama.
Kim Osborne, Executive Secretary for Integral Development, 
Organization of American States. 
Alejandra Castillo, Assistant Secretary of Commerce for Economic 
Development, U.S. Department of Commerce. 
Gina M. Raimondo, U.S. Secretary of Commerce (special remarks).
H.E. Francisco O. Mora, Ambassador, Permanent Representative, 
U.S. Department of State (Permanent Mission to the OAS).
Jorge Rivera Staff, Minister of Commerce and Industries of Panama. 
Distinguished Guest, H.E. Mari Carmen Aponte, 
U.S. Ambassador to Panama.
• Presentation of team ACE Panama 
• Cultural Exhibition
 6:30 p.m. - Departure to the hotel 
 Marriott Panama Hotel
 Dinner on your own
Local leaders and participants are encouraged 
to arrange networking dinners.

4
TUESDAY, JANUARY 30
 6:00 a.m. - Breakfast meetings with local 
stakeholders and participants at the hotel
• Courtesy breakfast at hotel. 
• Local stakeholders and delegates are encouraged to 
 schedule breakfast meetings ahead of time.
 7:20 a.m. - Meet at the Marriott Hotel Lobby
 7:30 a.m. - Transfer to the City of 
Knowledge
 8:00 a.m. - The City of Knowledge Opens its 
Doors to Explore Cooperation in Technology 
and Innovation 
A few minutes from downtown Panama City, the City of Knowledge is 
strategically located in front of the Panama Canal. Some 120 acres and 
more than 200 buildings of what was once the Clayton military base 
are now home to a thriving international community established for 
the purpose of collaborating in the business, academic, scientific and 
humanistic fields. The goal is knowledge-based human and sustainable 
development. 
• 8:00 a.m. Group Photo in the SENACYT (building 215). 
 
• 8:15 a.m. Guided Tour.
 8:45 a.m. - Transfer to 
 Holiday Inn Hotel – Coffee Break
 
 9:00 a.m. - Opening Remarks at Holiday Inn 
Hotel
Eduardo Ortega-Barria, National Secretary of Science, Technology and 
Innovation.
Jorge Arosemena Román, Executive President of the City of Knowledge 
Foundation. 
 9:45 a.m. - Transfer to POLESTAR and 
SILICE 
 10:00 a.m. - Visit to two technology and 
innovation institutions (POLESTAR and 
SILICE) 
The group will be split into two groups that will run simultaneously.
 11:20 a.m. - Transfer to Holiday Inn Hotel
 11:30 a.m. - Dynamic Panel on Technology and 
Innovation 
Panama’s key stakeholders will present how their institutions and 
companies are prioritizing innovation to succeed in the global market and 
improve the quality of life of their citizens. The discussion will be an open 
dialogue in which delegates are encouraged to share their perspectives and 
initiatives. 
• Panel “Technology and Innovation” 
Senator the Hon. Barry Griffin, Vice President of the Senate, Deputy 
Chairman of The Bahamas Trade Commission and Chief Trade Negotiator, 
The Bahamas.
Carlos Kan, National Director of Innovation, National Authority for 
Government Innovation (AIG).
Karin Sempf, Founder & CEO of Innova-Nation.
Marie Claire González, CEO, XPLOR.
Moderator: Omar Lopez, Technical Secretary, National Research System 
(SNI) of SENACYT.
 12:15 p.m. - Working lunch and networking at 
Holiday Inn Hotel 
Groups will be arranged to ensure local leaders and delegates have the 
opportunity to explore cooperation opportunities in areas of mutual 
interest.
 1:10 p.m. - Transfer to Panama Pacifico
 1:45 p.m. - Explore Panama Pacifico and 
Learn About Its Economic Opportunities 
Panama Pacifico is the largest mixed development in Latin America 
that connects people, companies and nature in a sustainable 
environment. The most complete Master Plan in Latin America, planned 
for 40 years with an investment of more than 879 million dollars and a 
land of 2,200 hectares, residential projects, 5 international schools, more 
than 365 prestigious companies, an international airport and more than 
600 green hectares. 
 
• Guided Tour of Panama Pacifico.
 2:20 p.m. - Learn First Hand About Business 
Opportunities 
Visit two prestigious businesses (H&M and PPG) focused on Logistics, 
Manufacturing and Innovation. The group will be split into two groups. 
Programs will run simultaneously.
 4:00 p.m. - Transfer to Panama Pacifico 
Customer Center
 4:20 p.m. - Panel “Opportunities for foreign 
direct investments”
Francisco Mola, Viceminister of Foreign Trade, MICI.
Carmen Gisela Vergara, Executive Director of PROPANAMA. 
Jeanette Díaz Granados, General Director of Multinational Company 
Headquarters, MICI.
Henry Kardonski, CEO of Panama Pacifico.
Jorge Rothery, Administrator of the Panama Pacific Agency.
Niurka Montero, General Manager at Dell Technologies Panama.
Moderator: Temístocles Rosas, President of the National Council of 
Private Enterprise (CoNEP).
 5:30 p.m. - Official Networking Cocktail event 
at the Panama Pacifico Visitor Center 
 6:30 p.m. - Departure to the Hotel 
 Marriott Panama Hotel
 Dinner on your own
Local leaders and participants are encouraged 
to arrange networking dinners.
Innovate-Tech Day: Pioneering the Future

5
WEDNESDAY, JANUARY 31
 6:00 a.m. - Breakfast meetings with local 
stakeholders and participants at the hotel
• Courtesy breakfast at hotel. 
• Local stakeholders and delegates are encouraged to 
 schedule breakfast meetings ahead of time.
 7:30 a.m. - Meet at the Marriott Hotel Lobby
 7:45 a.m. - Transfer to Ascanio Arosemena 
Complex of the Panama Canal Authority
 8:15 a.m. - Explore How Panama is Becoming an 
Energy Hub at the Management Wing of the 
Ascanio Arosemena Complex of the Panama 
Canal Authority 
Panama is already a maritime, air, and financial hub. The potential of becoming an 
energy hub has already been identified and explored for different purposes. This 
same geographical position lends itself to connecting the hydrogen production 
centers and the consumers with the highest demand.
• Keynote Conferences “Renewable Energy and Sustainable 
Water Use” 
Diana Laguna, Vice Minister of Environment. 
Rosilena Lindo, Executive Secretary of Energy. 
Juan A. Ducruet, General Director of the National Institute of Aqueducts and 
Sewers (IDAAN).
Moderator: Juan Pimento, Interim Executive Director – Hydroenvironmental Innovation, 
Research and Technology Center (CITEC). 
 9:30 a.m. - Transfer to Micro, Small and 
 Medium Enterprise Authority (AMPYME)
 10:15 a.m. - How are Entrepreneurs supported in 
Panama? 
Explore how the Micro, Small and Medium Enterprise Authority (AMPYME) is 
Promoting Sustainable Economic Growth in the Country through Start-Ups.
Speaker: Oscar Ramos, Director General of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprise 
Authority (AMPYME).
Host: Laru Linares, Manager of Women’s and Social Entrepreneurship, FCS. 
 10:30 a.m. - Be Ready for the Premier Innovation 
Showcase of the Americas – Innovation Exchange 
Coalition (IEC) 
Top tech entrepreneurs will present solutions and opportunities to explore business 
deals with the delegation. It will be a dynamic and fast-paced session, in which the 
delegation is encouraged to participate and be ready to connect with the entrepreneurs.
Sanna Gaspard, Rubitection, CEO, AI driven assessment and care management 
system supports the detection and management of chronic wounds.
Pedro Meneses, Aerialoop, CEO, Pioneer of a transformative urban drone 
delivery network to tackle the logistic inefficiencies of transporting small orders 
with large vehicles.
Jhosep Guzmán, WAPOSAT, CEO, Develops and installs cost-effective systems that 
monitor and control water quality in real-time.
Moderator: Natasha Main, Co-Founder, IEC.
 11:10 a.m. - “Meet the Entrepreneurs” - AMPYME - 
Exhibition and Networking with Panamanian 
Entrepreneurs 
Top Panamanian entrepreneurs will exhibit solutions and opportunities to 
explore business deals with the delegation. Delegation will be able to visit each 
entrepreneur’s stand and connect.
Host: Laru Linares, Manager of Women’s and Social Entrepreneurship, FCS. 
 12:15 p.m. - Transfer to the Technological 
 University of Panama
 1:15 p.m. - Lunch and Projects Presentations 
During lunch, the Technological University of Panama will present 
opportunities for cooperation.
 
• Welcome to UTP, Opening Remarks 
Omar Olmedo Aizpurúa, Rector of the Technological University of Panama. 
 
• 1:45 p.m. Project Presentations.
 
Dr. Danilo Cáceres, Intelligent Classification System for Citrullus Lanatus 
(Watermelon) for export using image processes. 
Dr. Cecilio Hernández, Chemical and mineralogical characterization of 
thermal springs with potential for tourism development in Panama.
 2:00 p.m. - Explore Research, Development, 
and Innovation Cooperation with the 
Technological University of Panama 
The Technological University of Panama (UTP) is the highest state institution 
in terms of scientific-technological higher education, in the Republic of 
Panama. Delegates will receive an introduction about the University and its 
programs and will have the opportunity to walk around the research labs 
and spaces used by entrepreneurs. 
 
Walking tour of the University will be provided, followed by visits and 
presentations of two projects being conducted at the University.
 
Dr. Yazmin Mack, Morbidity vs. water quality for Human Consumption in 
Tonosí: a pilot study. 
Dr. Mayteé Zambrano, Development of a hyperspectral image acquisition 
platform assisted by advanced machine learning techniques for suitability 
identification of rice crops.
 4:00 p.m. - Transfer to the City of Arts
 4:30 p.m. - Welcome to the City of Arts, 
 Opening Remarks
• 4:40 p.m. Welcome Remarks by Giselle González, Minister of Culture, 
Government of Panama
• 4:45 p.m. Group Photo at the City of Arts. 
 
• 4:50 p.m. Tour of the City of Arts.
 5:05 p.m. - “Education and Cooperation for 
 Innovation” Forum and Network
 Panamanian Academic Institutions will provide a brief overview of Panama’s 
 academic sector and the importance of Education for Innovation, followed 
 by a networking session where delegates are encouraged to engage with 
 local academic stakeholders.
Kathi Vidal, Under Secretary of Commerce for Intellectual Property 
and Director of the United States Patent and Trademark Office,
U.S. Department of Commerce. 
Milena Gómez Cedeño, Rector of ITSE.
Susana Lau Hou, President of the Institute of Electrical and 
Electronic Engineers.
Jose Cuervo Aguilar, Education Ambassador, GBM. 
Moderator: Gladys Bernett, Specialist in International Higher 
Education.
• 6:10 p.m. Refreshments and networking
 7:00 p.m. - Departure to the hotel 
 Marriott Panama Hotel
 Dinner on your own
Local leaders and participants are encouraged 
to arrange networking dinners.
The Energy and Entrepreneur Showcase Day

6
THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 1
Tourism and Energy Day - For a Greener and Sustainable Future
 6:00 a.m. - Grab and Go Breakfast for 
early departure 
Breakfast will be available at the hotel for early risers. Bagged 
breakfast will also be provided.
 6:20 a.m. - Meet at the Marriott Hotel 
Lobby
 6:30 a.m. - Transfer to the Wind Farm
 9:30 a.m. - Connect with Clean Energy 
and Renewable Energy Experts at the 
Laudato Sí Wind Farm 
This wind farm is located in the province of Coclé and it is the first 
wind farm in Panama, and the largest in Central America.
• Guided Tour of the Laudato Sí Wind Farm. 
• 10:20 a.m. Group Photo at the Laudato Sí Wind Farm. 
 
 10:30 a.m. - Transfer to 
 Royal Decameron Hotel
 
• 11:00 a.m. Group Photo at the Royal Decameron Hotel. 
 
 11:20 a.m. - “Agriculture and Tourism, 
Bolstering the Community and Region” 
This panel will provide delegates an insight into Panama’s Tourism”, 
Agriculture and Agro-tourism sector and their role in creating 
opportunities and progress for regions in Panama in a sustainable 
way, while empowering rural communities to continue seeking 
economic growth.
 
• Panel “Agriculture and Tourism, Bolstering the 
Community and Region” 
Magda Duran, Deputy Administrator of the Tourism Authority of 
Panama.
Mirhanna Sandoya, National Coordinator of the Agro-tourism 
Program, Competitiveness Center of the Western Region of 
Panama (CECOM-RO).
Samuel Valdés, President, Biodiversity Consultant Group.
 
 12:00 p.m. - Meet the Sustainable and 
Regional Tourism Entrepreneurs 
 12:45 p.m. - Networking Lunch 
Free conversation - mingle around and find partners.
 1:45 p.m. - Transfer to Finca la Polola, 
Pink Lion S.A., Chame
 2:45 p.m. - Visit Pink Lion S.A. and 
Experience First-Hand How Panama is 
Promoting Sustainable and Agro-tourism 
Pink Lion S.A. is an Agro-tourism project, oriented towards the 
production of quality exotic and tropical fruits, all of them certified and 
developed in improved soil, using environmentally friendly products 
and is seeking to expand its pitahaya business.
 4:30 p.m. - Departure to the Hotel 
 Marriott Panama Hotel
 6:30 p.m. - Arrival to the Hotel 
 Marriott Panama Hotel
 Dinner on your own
Local leaders and participants are encouraged 
to arrange networking dinners.

7
FRIDAY, FEBRUARY 2
 6:00 a.m. - Breakfast in Hotel 
Breakfast will be available at the hotel for early risers. Bagged breakfast will 
also be provided.
 7:00 a.m. - Meet at the Marriott Hotel Lobby 
 7:15 a.m. - Transfer to Panama Canal 
 Railway
 7:45 a.m. - Boarding the Panama Canal Railway, 
a trans-isthmian railway to Colón 
The route stretches 77 km across the Isthmus of Panama. When it opened 
in 1855 it was called the Interoceanic Railroad, which was later described as 
the first transcontinental railroad in the world.
 9:00 a.m. - Arrival to Colón Railway Station 
and Transfer to Agua Clara Visitor Center
 10:00 a.m. - Visit Agua Clara Visitor Center 
On one side you will find the famous Gatún Lake, where day after day cargo 
ships or other types of vessels transit through this magnificent work of 
modern engineering. You will be amazed by the view of the expansion work. 
• 10:00 a.m. Group Photo at Agua Clara Visitor Center. 
 
• 10:15 a.m. Guided tour of Agua Clara.
 11:10 a.m. - Transfer to Manzanillo 
 International Terminal (MIT)
 12:00 p.m. - “Integral Development of Colón” 
Panel at MIT-Panama
 Explore how the logistics sector serves as a potent economic catalyst, driving 
development and prosperity in this region. Gain insights from industry 
leaders on strategies to optimize infrastructure, foster strategic partnerships, 
and propel Colón towards a future of integrated growth and opportunity. 
Giovanni Ferrari Francucci, General Manager of the Colón Free Zone.
Esteban Barrientos, CEO, Generadora Gatún.
Lino Arosemena, CEO, TISA (Talleres Insustriales, S.A.).
Moderator: Josimar Ortiz, Executive Director at the Chamber of 
Commerce, Agriculture and Industries of Colón.
 12:45 p.m. - Networking Lunch 
Free conversation - mingle around and find partners.
 1:30 p.m. - Visit the Manzanillo International 
Terminal Simulators to Explore Panama’s 
Logistic Sector 
In August 1993, with the arrival of the multinational Stevedoring Services of 
America (now SSA Marine) to Panama, the original concept of the Ro-Ro 
dock became the container transshipment terminal it is today, with more 
than 1,600 meters of quay equipped with gantry cranes and modern 
computer management systems.
 2:30 p.m. - Transfer to buses and visit to 
the Port of the MIT
 3:30 p.m. - Transfer to BioMuseo
 5:00 p.m. - BioMuseo Tour 
A powerful blend of science and art, it is a natural history museum 
dedicated to Panama that explores the relationship between its 
biodiversity and culture. In 8 exhibitions, it tells the story of the 
emergence of the Isthmus of Panama 3 million years ago and how 
that emergence connected continents and separated seas, changing 
the world forever.
 
• 5:15 p.m. Group Photo at the BioMuseo. 
 
• 5:30 p.m. Guided tour to the BioMuseo. 
 6:00 p.m. - Speed-Business Meetings, Final 
Survey, and Defining the Road Map for 
Long-Term Partnerships 
Delegates will have the opportunity to re-connect with authorities, 
stakeholders and institutions they have connected through the 
days and have conversations on possible cooperation opportunities. 
The room will be set up with tables in which representatives from 
the government, private sector, and academia of Panama will be 
organized and will welcome delegates to the tables to advance 
conversations. Interactions will be limited to 15 minutes per delegate or 
group of delegates.
 6:30 p.m. - Closing Ceremony 
Remarks by the Government of Panama, the Organization of the 
American States, and the Government of the United States. 
• Certificates of Recognition for Participants 
 
• Official announcement of the U.S. ACE Spring 2024 
Host. Transition Ceremony to the next ACE Program 
in 2024 
 
• Interventions at Closing Ceremony 
Yill Otero, Vice Minister for Multilateral Affairs and Cooperation
Ministry of Foreign Affairs Government of Panama.
 7:30 p.m. - Departure to the hotel 
 Marriott Panama Hotel
 Dinner on your own
Local leaders and participants are encouraged 
to arrange networking dinners.
 
Connecting the Atlantic and Pacific - Panama, a Logistical Heaven

8
VISIT THE 
INFO CENTER
JANUARY 29 TO
FEBRUARY 2, 2024
